titlestmt.titleproper: First Union Baptist Church of Christ at Farmerville records6068
titlestmt.author: Compiled by S.L.

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-04-08 18:40:31 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: First Union Baptist Church of Christ at Farmerville records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: First Union Baptist Church of Christ at Farmerville (Interlaken, N.Y.)

did.abstract: One volume of covenant meeting minutes (1819-1894); one volume of annual meeting to elect trustees minutes (1825-1899); issues of the American Baptist Magazine and Missionary Intelligencer (Volume IV, no. 5; Volume III, no. 4); and minutes of the twentieth-seventh annual meeting of the Seneca Baptist Association (1848). Covenant meeting minutes document a split in 1828, accounts of revivals, church discipline and doctrine.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: This church was organized as the Baptist Church of Farmer Village on November 22, 1819 with Elder John Lewis as the pastor. A house of worship costing $3,000 was built in 1823. In 1828 a disagreement among members caused some to leave and join the Dutch Reformed Church at Farmerville. This rift was repaired in 1830 and the church called itself the Union Baptist Church thereafter, to signify the union of the two groups. Revivals led to rapid growth in the church's membership prior to the Civil War, and a new church, costing $8,000 was built in 1861. An unauthenticated source says that Captain Winfield Scott recruited 130 men from the church's Sunday School to serve in the Civil War and that their band played at Harper's Ferry and at Appomatox. The church was a member of the Seneca Association.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The collection contains one volume of Covenant meeting minutes (1819-1894), which contains membership lists for 1819 and 1830, one volume of trustee's records (1825-1899) which contains an account of the church's incorporation in 1824 and annual meeting minutes, The American Baptist Magazine, Vol. Ill no. 4 (July 1821) and Vol. IV no. 5, (Sept. 1823), and "Minutes of the Twenty-Seventh Annual Meeting of the Seneca Baptist Association" in 1848.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: First Union Baptist Church of Christ at Farmerville records, #6068. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: Seneca County (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.

Churches, Baptist.
Church records and registers -- New York (State) -- Interlaken.
Baptists -- Government.
Baptists -- New York (State) -- Interlaken.
controlaccess.title: American Baptist Magazine.
controlaccess.corpname: Baptist Church of Farmer Village (Interlaken, N.Y.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labeled "Church Clerk's Book". Contains the articles of faith of the church, meeting minutes, membership lists, and the church covenant. Items of note and approximate locations in the book follow:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles of Faith. The script version is covered by the printed version.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Meeting to decide on the propriety of founding a church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Church formed. 2 deeds for a pew from the trustees to Jeremiah Rappleye, the church clerk, dated Feb. 25, 1828 and Apr. 30, 1825.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discussion of church policy on fund raising.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Voted that the sisters not have an equal right with, the male members to vote in matters of discipline.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Church splits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anti-Masonry resolution. Resolution to aid foreign missions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attempts at reconciliation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Conference called. (Note: From pages 77-96 are the records of one group, then the records pass into the hands of the other group who recount their side of the story.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Declaration of the seceeders.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Account of the conference called.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Splinter group voted to join the Seneca Association.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Voted to hold a protracted meeting on the first Sunday in October.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report of the meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Voted to have the females hold a prayer meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Plans for a protracted meeting in October.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report of the meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Protracted meeting planned.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Voted to accept as members those who have been baptised by pedobaptist ministers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Voted to hold a protracted meeting with Elder King.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Met to organize the American Baptist Bible Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formed an Auxiliary Bible Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report of a protracted meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report of a protracted meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bro. expelled for embracing the Doctrine of Universal Salvation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Voted to circulate a subscription to buy a lot and build a parsonage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many baptisms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Protracted meeting led by 0. Montague

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many baptisms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discussion of building a new church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New house of worship dedicated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Voted to have Bro. George Balcom come and assist vith a protracted meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Revival.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sick man is baptised at home.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pagination ends. Last entry. Key to membership list.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Membership as of formation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Membership list (after split). Church Covenant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume includes minutes of yearly meeting to elect trustees and an account of the incorporation of the church on April 3, 1824 at the beginning of the volume, Items of note include:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolved to buy a lot and build a parsonage on it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Voted to tear down the old house of worship and build a new one.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discussion of pew rental.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Account of the church's financial situation and copy of the subscription list. (Note: After 1863 financial reports are included in the minutes.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolved to call the church the Baptist Church of Farmer Village.

unittitle.title: The American Baptist Magazine and Missionary Intelligencer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. III, No. 4, July 1821. Page 132 contains the report of a revival in Camillus, Onadago (sic.) County, New York in 1818.

unittitle.title: The American Baptist Magazine and Missionary Intelligencer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. IV, No. 5, Sept. 1823.

did.unittitle: Minutes of the Twenty-Seventh Annual Meeting of the Seneca Baptist Association

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For 1848. (Aug. 30, 31) held with the Baptist Church of Trumansburg, N.Y. Lists churches, clerks, delegates, number received by letter, dropped, died, membership, statistical information and correspondence with other associations with meeting minutes.
